masthead.title_html: Reason Your Way Through the Animal Kingdom
masthead.title_plain: Reason Your Way Through the Animal Kingdom
masthead.dek: Twenty questions where the answer always comes from a set you can count on your fingers — a country, a state, a body part, or a short list of beasts.
masthead.footer_html: Facts checked against conservation bodies, zoos, and reference encyclopedias · June 2026

questions.q: More than half of all wild Asian elephants live in one country. Which one?
questions.detail: India holds roughly 27,000 wild Asian elephants, about 55% of the world total.
questions.why: Asian elephants range across 13 countries, but India alone carries more than half — around 27,000 animals.
questions.more: No other range country breaks the low five figures, so this is not close.
questions.source: World Population Review, Elephant Population by Country 2026; IUCN range data
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [countries ~13 with Asian elephants]; reflex points at Africa for elephants so the Asian-only slicer narrows it; difficulty med

questions.q: Of the great apes — gorilla, orangutan, chimpanzee, bonobo, human — which non-human one is the smallest?
questions.detail: Adult male bonobos stand about 73–83 cm tall and weigh 37–61 kg, the lightest of the non-human great apes.
questions.why: People reach for the gibbon, but gibbons are lesser apes; among the great apes the slender bonobo is the smallest, topping out near 60 kg.
questions.more: Gorillas, the largest, can pass 200 kg — more than triple a big bonobo.
questions.source: Discover Wildlife, Great Ape Guide; IFAW ape conservation facts
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the great apes ~5]; reflex-defeater: gibbon is excluded as a lesser ape; difficulty med

questions.q: How many hearts does an octopus have?
questions.state: Three (two gill hearts plus one main heart)
questions.detail: An octopus has three hearts: two branchial hearts pump blood through the gills, and one systemic heart pushes it to the body.
questions.why: Blue, copper-based blood carries oxygen poorly, so the body-plan logic is 'add pumps' — two for the gills, one for everything else.
questions.more: The systemic heart pauses when the octopus swims, which is why they prefer to crawl.
questions.source: Britannica, How Many Hearts Does an Octopus Have; Discover Wildlife
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [small guessable count]; reasoning path from oxygen-poor blue blood; difficulty med

questions.q: There are eight species of bear. Which is the smallest?
questions.detail: The sun bear of Southeast Asia grows to about 5 feet long and up to 150 lb, roughly half an American black bear.
questions.why: The eight bears are a short list to recite; the tropical, tree-climbing sun bear is the runt, dwarfed even by the panda.
questions.more: Its long tongue and small size suit a forest diet of insects, honey, and fruit.
questions.source: Britannica, Sun bear; WWF Sun Bear profile
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the eight bears]; reflex-defeater removes the famous big bears (polar, grizzly); difficulty med

questions.q: Of Africa's Big Five — lion, leopard, elephant, rhino, buffalo — which kills the most people?
questions.detail: The Cape buffalo, especially when wounded, is the deadliest of the official Big Five, killing an estimated 200 people a year.
questions.why: Hippos kill more, but hippos are not in the Big Five; inside that named set the unpredictable buffalo, not the lion, tops the list.
questions.more: Hunters nicknamed it 'the Black Death' for ambushing those who track it.
questions.source: African Budget Safaris; AfricaHunting, How Dangerous Are the Big Five
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [Africa's Big Five]; slicer removes the off-list hippo and the reflex lion; difficulty hard

questions.q: Which U.S. state has the most moose?
questions.detail: Alaska holds roughly 175,000 moose, far ahead of any other state.
questions.why: Moose need cold and boreal forest, and Alaska has the most of both — about 175,000 animals.
questions.more: Maine, the leader in the lower 48, has only around 60,000.
questions.source: A-Z Animals, States With the Most Moose; World Deer
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [50 states]; cold-climate body-plan reasoning; difficulty med

questions.q: Setting Alaska aside, which U.S. state has the most wild wolves?
questions.detail: Minnesota has about 2,700 wild wolves in roughly 500 packs, the most in the lower 48.
questions.why: Alaska dominates with 7,000–11,000, so removing it makes this real; the upper-Midwest stronghold of Minnesota then wins.
questions.more: Its wolves never fully disappeared, which is why they outnumber any reintroduced western population.
questions.source: International Wolf Center, Minnesota; World Population Review, Wolf Population by State
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [50 states]; explicit slicer removes runaway leader Alaska; difficulty hard

questions.q: Which country has the most wild giraffes?
questions.detail: Kenya is home to about 34,000 giraffes, the largest national population.
questions.why: Giraffes survive best in East Africa's savannas, and Kenya's mix of reserves and species gives it the top count, ahead of South Africa.
questions.more: Kenya and Tanzania together hold most of the world's Masai and reticulated giraffes.
questions.source: Giraffe Conservation Foundation, State of Giraffe 2025; National Geographic
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [African range countries ~15]; reflex-defeater: South Africa is the safari cliché but not the leader; difficulty hard

questions.q: Which penguin species lives the farthest north — the only one that reaches the equator?
questions.detail: The Galapagos penguin is the only penguin found naturally north of the equator.
questions.why: Penguins read as 'cold and south,' but a cold equatorial current lets this small species nest on islands straddling the line.
questions.more: The Cromwell Current's cool, food-rich water is what makes equatorial penguins possible.
questions.source: Wikipedia, Galapagos penguin; Metropolitan Touring, Equatorial Tropical Penguin
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [penguin species ~18]; reflex-defeater: penguins assumed Antarctic; difficulty hard

questions.q: How many bones are in a giraffe's neck?
questions.detail: A giraffe's neck has seven cervical vertebrae — the same number as a human's.
questions.why: Almost every mammal has exactly seven neck bones; the giraffe just stretched each one to about 10 inches.
questions.more: Length, not extra bones, is the trick — and humans share the same count.
questions.source: Snopes, Giraffes Neck Bones Humans; Save Giraffes Now
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [small count]; reflex-defeater: people expect many bones for a long neck; difficulty med

questions.q: Which U.S. state has the most American bison?
questions.detail: South Dakota holds roughly 30,000 bison, the most of any state.
questions.why: The southern Great Plains and big reserves like Custer State Park push South Dakota past Montana's ~19,000.
questions.more: Tribal herds add heavily to South Dakota's total.
questions.source: A-Z Animals, States With the Most Bison; Custer State Park
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [50 states]; reflex-defeater: Yellowstone makes people guess Wyoming/Montana; difficulty hard

questions.q: A cow's stomach is divided into how many compartments?
questions.detail: A cow has a single stomach with four compartments: rumen, reticulum, omasum, and abomasum.
questions.why: The common 'four stomachs' line is wrong — it is one stomach, four chambers, built to ferment tough grass.
questions.more: The rumen alone holds the microbes that break down cellulose for most of the cow's energy.
questions.source: Britannica, How Many Stomachs Does a Cow Have; MSU Extension
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [small count]; reflex-defeater: 'four stomachs' myth reframed as four chambers; difficulty med

questions.q: Mountain gorillas live in just three countries. Which one has the most?
questions.detail: Uganda holds more than half the world's mountain gorillas, about 459 of roughly 1,000.
questions.why: Only Uganda, Rwanda, and the DRC have them, and Uganda's Bwindi forest carries the largest share.
questions.more: Bwindi Impenetrable Forest alone accounts for most of Uganda's gorillas.
questions.source: World Population Review, Gorilla Population by Country 2026; 2019 mountain gorilla census
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the 3 mountain-gorilla countries]; tiny named set walked directly; difficulty hard

questions.q: Of the eight bears, which is the only one whose diet is almost all plants?
questions.detail: The giant panda eats bamboo for about 99% of its diet, unique among the eight bears.
questions.why: Every other bear is an omnivore or hunter; the panda has a carnivore's gut but lives almost entirely on bamboo.
questions.more: Pandas can digest meat but rarely bother, eating bamboo for many hours a day instead.
questions.source: Science (AAAS), How Pandas Survive on Bamboo; Live Science
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the eight bears]; identity pick on a unique trait; difficulty med

questions.q: Which great ape is the only one not native to Africa?
questions.detail: Orangutans are the only great apes native to Asia, living only on Borneo and Sumatra.
questions.why: Gorillas, chimps, and bonobos are all African; the orangutan is the lone Asian holdout, which is why it stands apart.
questions.more: Three orangutan species share those two Indonesian and Malaysian islands.
questions.source: WWF, Where Do Orangutans Live; GRID-Arendal great apes range
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the great apes]; slicer on geography removes the African majority; difficulty med

questions.q: Which country has the most wild orangutans?
questions.detail: Indonesia holds the large majority of wild orangutans, including roughly 100,000 in Borneo's Kalimantan plus Sumatra's populations.
questions.why: Orangutans live only on Borneo and Sumatra; most of Borneo is Indonesian Kalimantan, so Indonesia far outweighs Malaysia.
questions.more: Malaysia's Sabah and Sarawak together hold only around 13,000.
questions.source: WCS Malaysia, Orangutan; conservation population estimates
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the 2 orangutan range countries]; reasoning from island geography; difficulty med

questions.q: Roughly two-thirds of the world's polar bears live in one country. Which one?
questions.detail: Canada is home to about 16,000 polar bears, roughly two-thirds of the global total of around 26,000.
questions.why: Polar bears ring the Arctic, but Canada owns the most Arctic coastline and sea ice, so it carries most of the bears.
questions.more: Canada manages 13 of the world's polar bear subpopulations.
questions.source: World Population Review, Polar Bear Population by Country; Environment and Climate Change Canada
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the ~5 Arctic countries]; reflex-defeater: Russia/Arctic assumed, but coastline favors Canada; difficulty hard

questions.q: Which of the eight bears is the only one classed as a marine mammal?
questions.detail: The polar bear is the only bear officially classified as a marine mammal, depending on sea ice for food and habitat.
questions.why: It hunts seals from the ocean's ice and even has the Latin name Ursus maritimus, 'sea bear.'
questions.more: It is still the least aquatic of all marine mammals, relying on ice rather than open water.
questions.source: U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service, Polar Bear; Marine Mammal Commission
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [the eight bears]; identity on a surprising legal classification; difficulty med

questions.q: Of Africa's Big Five, which animal has the smallest wild population?
questions.detail: The critically endangered black rhino numbers just over 3,000 in the wild, the fewest of the Big Five.
questions.why: Lions, leopards, elephants, and buffalo all run far higher; poaching cut the black rhino from over a million to a few thousand.
questions.more: The white rhino, a separate species, is far more numerous — so the question hinges on which rhino.
questions.source: African Budget Safaris, The Big Five; Save the Rhino population figures
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [Africa's Big Five]; slicer between rhino species sharpens it; difficulty hard

questions.q: How many toes does an ostrich have on each foot?
questions.detail: The ostrich is the only bird with just two toes on each foot; all other birds have three or four.
questions.why: Fewer toes mean a longer stride and less drag, helping the ostrich hold 30+ mph on open ground.
questions.more: The larger toe carries a long claw that adds grip and a dangerous kick.
questions.source: Britannica, Ostrich; San Diego Zoo Wildlife Explorers
questions._mechanism: answer-domain routed to [small count]; reflex-defeater: most assume three or four toes like other birds; difficulty hard
